rationale: The citing paper uses this reference to make a concrete supply-side point: biased state-dependence estimates can distort firms' dynamic pricing incentives and equilibrium pricing conclusions. It cites Dube, Hitsch, and Rossi (2009) specifically when discussing these implications, and later comments on whether its corrected loyalty estimates would change that paper's main pricing result. This goes beyond a passing background citation and functions as a meaningful qualification of prior findings.
relation_description: The citing paper refines the interpretation of Dube et al. (2009) by arguing that improved correction for initial conditions changes estimated loyalty magnitudes that feed into dynamic pricing analysis. It then qualifies that the cited paper's qualitative conclusion may still hold, while highlighting sensitivity of equilibrium implications to demand-side bias.
